question: Which countries have both high rice production (Tons) and high rice yield (Kg / Hectare)?

analysis.design_rationale: A scatter plot is ideal for visualizing the relationship between rice production volume and rice yield per hectare across all countries. By plotting both metrics for each country, high producers and high yielders appear in the top-right quadrant. This approach directly exposes which countries combine high output with high productivity and spotlights those excelling in both metrics. Annotation and color highlighting further guide viewers to the top-performing nations.
analysis.target_insight: Identify countries that rank highly in both rice production and rice yield, distinguishing them visually and making it easy to spot global leaders who excel in both volume and agricultural efficiency.

chart_encodings.color: Highlight countries above 75th percentile in both metrics (distinct color), others as gray
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: annotate highlighted countries, alpha for scatter points

transformations: Convert df['Rice Production (Tons)'] from string abbreviations (K, M) to numeric value
Convert df['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)'] from string to float
Calculate 75th percentile threshold for rice production and rice yield
Create boolean mask for countries above both thresholds
data_preparation_steps: Define helper function to parse K/M notation to numeric
Apply function to df['Rice Production (Tons)'] and assign to new column 'Rice Production (Tons)_num'
Convert df['Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)'] to float and assign to new column 'Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)_num'
Create mask for countries above both percentiles
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\3.csv")

def parse_km(val):
    val = str(val).strip()
    if val[-1] == "K":
        return float(val[:-1]) * 1e3
    elif val[-1] == "M":
        return float(val[:-1]) * 1e6
    else:
        try:
            return float(val)
        except:
            return np.nan

df["Rice Production (Tons)_num"] = df["Rice Production (Tons)"].apply(parse_km)
df["Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)_num"] = df["Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)"].astype(float)

prod_thresh = df["Rice Production (Tons)_num"].quantile(0.75)
yield_thresh = df["Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)_num"].quantile(0.75)

mask = (df["Rice Production (Tons)_num"] >= prod_thresh) & (df["Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)_num"] >= yield_thresh)

df_top = df[mask]

plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
plt.scatter(df["Rice Production (Tons)_num"], df["Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)_num"], color="gray", alpha=0.6, label="Other countries")
plt.scatter(df_top["Rice Production (Tons)_num"], df_top["Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)_num"], color="orange", s=80, label="High production and yield")
for idx, row in df_top.iterrows():
    plt.text(row["Rice Production (Tons)_num"]+0.02*prod_thresh, row["Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)_num"], row["Country"], fontsize=9, color="black")
plt.xlabel("Rice Production (Tons)")
plt.ylabel("Rice Yield (Kg / Hectare)")
plt.title("Countries with both high rice production and high rice yield\n(Top quartile highlighted)")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
